authoring_model.ownership: This section owns the target skill's SKILL.md facade contract.
authoring_model.facade_constraint: SKILL.md may route readers and state stable boundaries only; deep governance must live in routing or topic docs.

required_read_sequence: references/routing/PROFILE_ROUTING.md when profile selection is required
routing_protocol.machine_rule_source: Cli_Toolbox JSON outputs plus generated machine-readable contracts
routing_protocol.markdown_role: human audit, semantic routing, and anchor-based narrowing

basic.target_shape: single-axis skill control plane with facade, task routing, and minimal governance atoms

staged_cli_first.target_shape: staged skill control plane with facade, routing, governance atoms, runtime contract, and stage contract kit

tool_contracts.Cli_Toolbox.create_skill_from_template: Create or refactor a skill from the governed template pack.
tool_contracts.Cli_Toolbox.skill_template: Return the basic facade template that already assumes doc-structure governance.
tool_contracts.Cli_Toolbox.staged_skill_template: Return the staged facade template that routes readers into stage contracts.
tool_contracts.Cli_Toolbox.contract_reference: Return the authoring contract for governed skills.
tool_contracts.Cli_Toolbox.staged_skill_reference: Return the generalized staged profile reference.
tool_contracts.Cli_Toolbox.runtime_contract_template: Return the staged runtime contract template asset.
tool_contracts.Cli_Toolbox.architecture_playbook: Return the architecture playbook for skill authoring.
tool_contracts.Cli_Toolbox.runtime_contract: Return this skill's own runtime contract.

asset_contracts.generated_output_expectation: generated skills must be facade-first, doc-structure-complete, and directly editable without post-generation structural rescue

governance_rules: SKILL.md must remain an entry-only facade, not a mixed governance body.
The facade contract of the target skill is governed here by SkillsManager-Creation-Template.
SkillsManager-Doc-Structure is mandatory for both new-skill creation and existing-skill governance.
SkillsManager-Doc-Structure starts from the entry handoff below SKILL.md and governs the downstream document tree.
Keep the primary reading path as facade -> routing -> topic atom before adding graph cross-links.
The existing 技能本体 / 规则说明 convention may survive only in routing or topic docs where it remains semantically local.
If a skill has runtime rules, the model must consume machine-readable contracts through CLI outputs instead of treating markdown as the runtime rule source.
staged_cli_first skills must explicitly define resident docs, stage order, stage checklist, stage doc contract, stage command contract, stage graph contract, and stage-switch discard policy.
Template clusters must separate human-readable markdown anchors from machine-readable contracts.
Prefer whole-template rewrites over patch stacking when the architecture contract changes.
When template governance changes, update facade, runtime contracts, routing docs, references, assets, generator logic, tooling docs, and regression checks together.
validation_closure: generate at least one basic skill sandbox
generate at least one staged skill sandbox
run SkillsManager-Doc-Structure lints on generated sandboxes
verify staged assets include stage checklist and contract quartet
keep tooling docs and regression tests in sync with generator behavior
notes: The skill now treats SkillsManager-Doc-Structure as an explicit mandatory contract.
